content.value: To ask someone what they do for a living, you can ask 你做咩呀？ (nei5 zou6 me1 aa3?), which literally means 'You do what?'. A slightly more complete version is 你做咩嘢呀？ (nei5 zou6 me1 je5 aa3?).

content.translation: Do what? / What do you do?

content.value: Here are two common professions to start with: 學生 (hok6 saang1 - student) and 先生 (sin1 saang1 - teacher). Note that '先生' can also mean 'Mister' or 'husband' depending on the context!

lines.speaker: A
lines.romanization: nei5 hou2! nei5 zou6 me1 aa3?

lines.speaker: B
lines.romanization: ngo5 hai6 hok6 saang1. nei5 ne1?
lines.translation: I am a student. What about you?

pages.title: Professions Mastered!
pages.summary: You can now talk about what people do for work.
